t_ch: 这我就插不上话了…
t_en: Don't think I have the qualifications to advise here...

t_ch: 家庭教育这题，只有杨叔会做…
t_en: When it comes to family stuff, I leave it to Mr. Yang...

t_ch: 其实我妈是个通情达理的人。可一谈到飞行的话题，她就变得不可理喻了。
t_en: My mom is actually a reasonable person, but she gets unreasonable as soon as I mention flying.

t_ch: 藏起我的星槎玩具，把呆在港口数飞船的我拉走，逼着我坐在桌案前读书。虽然我费尽心思最后进了天舶司，结果在她的安排下，我却干起了文职。
t_en: She hid my starskiff toys, dragged me away from the port when I stayed behind to count ships, forced me to study for ages... I did everything I could to work my way into the Sky-Faring Commission, but she used her authority to assign me a desk job.

t_ch: 唉，每天走过宣夜大街，抬头看那些自由自在划过天空的飞行器，心里就空落落的……
t_en: *sigh* Every time I walk along Starwatcher Avenue and look up at those ships flying freely across the sky, I feel empty inside...

t_ch: 不好意思啊，肚子里也是。刚看到我妈招待你们的那什么茶点，味道好不好啊？
t_en: Hehe, speaking of empty inside... So how were Mom's refreshments? Good?

t_ch: 嗯！这浓郁的味道不可思议…
t_en: Yes! The aroma was incredible...

t_ch: 别硬撑了，一口解千愁。
t_en: Help yourself, I'm sure there's some leftover.

t_ch: 唉，今天闹了这么一阵，都气饱了，啥也没吃上。
t_en: *sigh* I've been so angry today I forgot to eat anything.

t_ch: 不如这样，我妈招待了各位上好的茶点，我也不能没什么表示。我带两位逛逛星槎海，尝尝罗浮本地最有名的小吃！
t_en: How about this? My mom thanked you guys with refreshments, so I should do the same! Let me take you around Starskiff Haven — we can try the most authentic snack on the Luofu!

t_ch: 到了！仙舟罗浮最地道的小吃…其实是饮料啦！就在这个自动售货机里。
t_en: We're here! The thing about the most authentic snack on the Xianzhou Luofu is... it's a drink! And it's inside this vending machine.

t_ch: 真是有够简陋的招待…
t_en: Wow, the full five-star treatment...

t_ch: 对不起！我一个坐办公室的，实在请不起你们吃大餐。
t_en: I'm sorry! I'm afraid I don't have the money for anything fancy.

t_ch: 又是茶？司辰宫里喝很多啦？
t_en: Tea again? I got enough in the Palace of Astrum.

t_ch: 不不，这次是风味特产。
t_en: No, no, it's a local specialty, trust me.

t_ch: 你看这个，苏打豆汁，一人来一罐。
t_en: Here. Mung bean soda. One can each.

t_ch: 虽然这个东西的味道也许有些独特，但我超喜欢喝。
t_en: This thing has a bit of a unique flavor, but I love it.

t_ch: 让我来尝尝这个。嗯，味道令人怀念。
t_en: Let me try. *glug* Hmm, a nostalgic experience.

t_ch: 唔，您的家乡也出产类似的风味饮料吗？
t_en: Oh? Is there a drink like this where you're from?

t_ch: 不，这一口让我想起了刚登上列车时…艰难吞下姬子泡的咖啡…食道受伤的感觉。
t_en: No, no, this reminds me of when I first boarded the Express... and forced myself to drink Himeko's coffee... and how my tastebuds never fully recovered.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，怎么样？你还喝得惯吗？
t_en: How is it, {NICKNAME}? Bearable?

t_ch: 好喝，太好喝了！
t_en: Delicious! Simply delicious!

t_ch: 哇，第一次见到能痛饮苏打豆汁的异邦人！
t_en: Whoa, I've never met an outworlder who could down a mung bean soda like that!

t_ch: 哕…味道可怕…
t_en: *retch* ...Anyone have a bucket...?

t_ch: 我第一次喝也被呛到了。但是再多喝几口，就有种说不清道不明的滋味盘旋在唇齿间，突然就爱上了这豆汁的怪味儿。
t_en: D—Don't worry, I choked the first time too! Have a few more sips, it takes a while for the unique flavor to, uh... work.

t_ch: 这是报酬？是报仇吧？
t_en: Are you sure you're not angry at us for rescuing you?

t_ch: 对、对不起！
t_en: Th—That bad? Sorry!

t_ch: 唉，自己喜欢的东西，果然并不是每个人都能接受的呀，就像飞行一样……
t_en: But I guess not everyone can accept what I like. *sigh* Just like flying...

t_ch: 喝不下可千万别勉强了。咱们到别处去看看吧，我知道星槎海有个地方特别适合观赏星槎。
t_en: Well, don't force yourself. Let's go somewhere else — I know a really great place in Starskiff Haven to starskiff-watch!

t_ch: 其实人生就是这样的，没人清楚打开豆汁罐子前，自己会是喜欢还是讨厌。独一无二的体验往往就藏在这种带有风险的抉择里。
t_en: Hehe, y'know, there are lot of "mung bean soda moments" in life — if you don't try you won't know. Unique experiences are often a product of risky choices.

t_ch: 哈，瓦尔特先生和我妈真是不一样。她总和我说：「一旦踏上天空就无法回头了，飞行士可是和死亡共舞的。」
t_en: Ha, tell that to my mom. Her number one life lesson is: "You can't turn back once you enter the sky. Pilots are always dancing with death."

t_ch: 我知道她是对的，我知道斗舰飞行士是劳累又危险的工作。可我还是想在星间飞行，在辽阔的银河中为仙舟联盟而战……
t_en: I know she's right — I know being a fighter pilot is an exhausting and dangerous job... but I still want to fly among the stars — to fight for the Xianzhou Alliance across the vast galaxy...

t_ch: 晴霓小姐为什么会执着于这个梦想呢？
t_en: Why are you so dedicated to that idea, Miss Qingni?

t_ch: 你们知道狐人的「试儿」习俗吗？就是在孩子出生不久，在她周围摆上一圈代表未来命运的物品。
t_en: Have you heard of the foxian "birthfate" tradition? Soon after a child is born, we surround them with items that represent future destinies.

t_ch: 抓住玉兆，多半意味着孩子将来会成为卜者；抓住刀剑，那么成为战士建功立业指日可待；抓住星槎玩具……
t_en: If the child reaches for a jade abacus, then they'll grow up to be a diviner. If they reach for a sword, then they'll grow up to be a famous warrior. And if they reach for a toy starskiff...

t_ch: 就会成为玩具推销员？
t_en: They'll become a toy salesman?

t_ch: 什么呀都是…是预示孩子会走上飞行士的道路。
t_en: What? No... It means they'll become a pilot.

t_ch: 就会成为星槎飞行士？
t_en: They'll become a starskiff pilot?

t_ch: 你猜的一点儿也没错。
t_en: That's right!

t_ch: 莫非你选了星槎？
t_en: So you reached for the starskiff?

t_ch: 不，不是我。是我那天才无双的妈妈。
t_en: No, not me... My genius mom did...

t_ch: 妈妈小时候选中了星槎，她果然展现出了极高的飞行天赋。才刚成年，就被景元将军招进了云骑军，成为了一名斗舰飞行士。
t_en: Mom reached for the starskiff and showed a natural talent for flying. General Jing Yuan recruited her to the Cloud Knights as soon as she was old enough — that's how she became a fighter pilot.

t_ch: 妈妈创造过无数奇迹…她曾在步离人密集的防空火力中，如同箭矢穿过暴雨般畅行无阻，一连击坠三艘兽舰。她还是天舶司竞速纪录，击坠数纪录的保持者。
t_en: Later, she achieved miracles... One time, she destroyed three beast ships in a hail of borisin anti-aircraft fire — like an arrow tearing through a thunderstorm... She also holds the Sky-Faring Commission records for speed and enemy vessels destroyed.

t_ch: 作为驭空大人的女儿，我很自豪。打从懂事起，我就希望未来能像她那样成为传奇。
t_en: I'm proud to be Madam Yukong's daughter. I've wanted to become a legend like her for as long as I can remember.

t_ch: 可以想象。父母总是孩子最初的英雄，而英雄的形象往往会影响孩子的一生。
t_en: I can imagine. A parent is often a child's first hero, and their image can influence their whole life.

t_ch: 虽然这些故事都不是她讲给我听的。打从第三次丰饶民战争结束后到现在，她再也没有登上过星槎，甚至绝口不提自己的战绩。
t_en: *sigh* She never told me any of those stories herself, and she hasn't piloted a starskiff since the Third Denizens of Abundance War. She doesn't even bring up her military achievements.

t_ch: 小时候我有样学样偷开星槎，实验各种极限操作…事实证明我的确像她一样有天赋，因为我不但活了下来，还停得很稳。
t_en: I used to watch her fly when I was young... then one time, I stole a starskiff and I tried all kinds of difficult maneuvers. Turns out I was as talented as she was. Not only did I survive the flight, my landing was perfect.

t_ch: 当时，我被拎到妈妈面前的时候，还开心地向她炫耀，以为她会哈哈大笑摸着我脑袋说不愧是我的女儿。结果，唉，我从没见过她表情这么可怕…
t_en: I remember feeling so happy when they took me to see Mom — there I was boasting about the flight, expecting her to laugh, pat my head, say "that's my girl" or something. But boy, I've never seen her so scary...

t_ch: 我承认，未成年人驾驶星槎，该打。只是，妈妈当时似乎不仅是为这事愤怒，而是为我以她为榜样这件事而愤怒，为我有和她一样的天赋而愤怒。我一直想不通为什么。
t_en: I admit, I deserved to be punished — you can't let a kid get away with stealing a starskiff... but my Mom was angry about more than that. She was angry because I viewed her as a role model — because I had the same talent as her. I don't understand it.

t_ch: 父母不想让孩子涉险。驭空大人的心思，我想我能理解。
t_en: Parents never want their children to be in danger. I think I can understand Madam Yukong.

t_ch: 可是…飞行，是我唯一想做的事情。因为我已经触碰过天空了。
t_en: But... flying is the only thing I want to do. I've already touched the sky.

t_ch: 晴霓收到了一条短信，皱着眉头地读了起来。
t_en: Qingni receives a message and frowns as she reads it.

t_ch: 刚才…妈妈的秘书，夕葵小姐给我发短信…说之前在搭救我时，妈妈有一个随身的挂饰遗落在了迴星港……
t_en: It's Mom's secretary, Miss Xikui... She just sent me a message — Mom lost one of her trinkets in Stargazer Navalia when she came to save me...

t_ch: 夕葵小姐建议我，将那个挂饰找回来还给妈妈，也借此修复一下和她的关系。
t_en: Miss Xikui suggested I find the trinket and return it to Mom — said it might help me get back in her good books.

t_ch: 遗落在迴星港吗？嗯…那我们陪你走一遭，一起找找看吧。
t_en: In Stargazer Navalia? Mm... Let's go with you and have a look.

t_ch: 这样劳烦二位，真的合适吗？
t_en: Is it really okay to bother you two again?

t_ch: 我们列车的宗旨就是乐于助人。
t_en: The goal of the Express Crew is to help other people.

t_ch: 正是如此。
t_en: Indeed.

t_ch: 二位恩公对我真是太好了，下次一定要请你们吃很多好吃的！
t_en: You're too kind, benefactors. I'll treat you to something better than mung bean soda next time!

t_ch: 就当是回报你的苏打豆汁了。
t_en: Our way of saying thanks for the mung bean soda.

t_ch: 对、对不起，别提豆汁了！
t_en: S—Sorry! Please don't mention the mung bean soda again!

t_ch: 和孽物战斗时一片混乱，妈妈的东西应该就丢在了附近。
t_en: The fight with the abominations was pretty chaotic... I think Mom's trinket would've dropped around here.

t_ch: 晴霓小姐知道那个挂饰什么样吗？
t_en: Do you know what it looks like, Miss Qingni?

t_ch: 我想想…应该是一个方方正正的小物件？我记得她一直贴身带着这样一个挂饰。
t_en: Lemme think... It should be a little square thing? She's usually got something like that on her.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，看来到你的专业领域了。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, this is your area of expertise.

t_ch: 我的专业领域不是战斗吗？
t_en: Isn't combat my expertise?

t_ch: 你当然是个优秀的战士，但你敏锐的观察力更加令我叹服。无论宝箱藏得有多深，你都能把它找出来。
t_en: You're a great fighter, but your perception is even more impressive. No matter how well a treasure chest is hidden, you're always able to spot it...

t_ch: 难道还有人比你{NICKNAME}更擅长从满地的杂物中找到一个不起眼的挂饰吗？
t_en: ...so, {NICKNAME}, when it comes to finding a needle in a haystack, is there really anyone more capable than yourself?

t_ch: 我的专业领域不是解谜吗？
t_en: Isn't solving puzzles my expertise?

t_ch: 从满地的杂物中找到一个不起眼的挂饰，这不正是一种解谜吗？
t_en: Well, isn't finding a needle in a haystack a type of puzzle-solving?

t_ch: 无论宝箱藏得有多深，你都能把它找出来。你敏锐的观察力总是令我叹服，这件事交给你来做，我绝对放心。
t_en: Your powers of perception are impressive — no matter how well a treasure chest is hidden, you're always able to spot it... I think you're the right person for the job.

t_ch: {F#本小姐}{M#在下}，正是谛听！
t_en: Who do you think I am, Diting!?

t_ch: 你敏锐的观察力总是令我叹服，这件事交给你来做，我是绝对放心的。
t_en: Your powers of perception are impressive, that's all — I think you're the right person for the job.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，别开玩笑了。都说了是个挂饰。
t_en: Come on {NICKNAME}, are you kidding? It's a trinket, remember?

t_ch: 我妈怎么会把这样一个东西「贴身携带」呢！
t_en: You think my mom would carry this with her?

t_ch: 但这瓷瓶本身倒是很精致漂亮，这款式应该是「仙兽祥瑞纹」吧？
t_en: It's a beautiful vase though — what an intricate pattern. Isn't that the Divine Beast Fortune pattern?

t_ch: 瓦尔特先生真是博学多识！
t_en: You're so smart, Mr. Yang!

t_ch: 我也是现学现卖罢了。
t_en: Well, I only recently found out about it.

t_ch: 阵刀？这不会也是之前遗落的东西吧？当时也来了不少云骑。
t_en: A devastator glaive? Is this from the battle? There were a lot of Cloud Knights.

t_ch: 不，这个只是模型…应该是拍摄幻戏要用的道具吧？
t_en: No, this is only a replica... Probably some kind of film prop.

t_ch: 鸟笼啊…突然想起，永狩原有一种鸟，有着漂亮的羽翼，但无法被人驯养。因为只要将它们关在笼中，它们便活不了多久。
t_en: A birdcage... Huh, that reminds me. Have you guys heard of the birds that live in the Ever-Hunt Plains? They have beautiful feathers but you can't domesticate them. They don't live long if you lock them up in a cage.

t_ch: 触碰过天空的鸟，是没办法在笼中生活的。
t_en: Makes sense... How could a bird that's known the sky live in a cage.

t_ch: 这应该不是驭空司舵遗落的东西，实在太大了。
t_en: Well, this is too big for a trinket.

t_ch: 有了！就是这个！这就是妈妈的挂饰…嗯？里面似乎有个东西？
t_en: Yes! This is it! This is Mom's trinket... Huh? There's something inside...

t_ch: 这是妈妈一直贴身携带的挂饰，但我以前从没注意到，里面还藏了个小东西……
t_en: Mom always carries this trinket around with her, but I never realized there was something inside...

t_ch: 这是…巡猎的徽记？
t_en: Is this... the Hunt crest?

t_ch: 现代艺术？
t_en: Modern art?

t_ch: 护身符？
t_en: A lucky charm?

t_ch: 搞不太懂…
t_en: I don't get it...

t_ch: 这是飞行士会挂在星槎舱室里用来祈求飞行好运的小东西。
t_en: This is what pilots hang in their starskiff cockpits for good luck.

t_ch: 这一定是驭空司舵相当珍重的东西。晴霓小姐，我们送你回去，请你亲自将它还给令堂吧。也借此契机，和她好好聊聊。
t_en: It must be very close to Helm Master Yukong's heart. Let's head back, Miss Qingni — I think you should return this trinket in person. This is an opportunity for the two of you to talk.

t_ch: 不，我想等一下再回去。在此之前，有件事希望二位恩公能帮我。
t_en: ...Not yet, I want to go back later. There's something I hope you two can help me with first.

t_ch: 如果是让我们帮你劝说令堂，这恐怕有些困难。清官难断家务事，我们终究是外人。
t_en: If you want us to help you persuade your mother, I'm afraid that could be difficult. It's not easy to solve a family dispute as an outsider...

t_ch: 您误会了，我只是…想知道母亲究竟在想什么。
t_en: You misunderstand, I just... wanna know what she's thinking.

t_ch: 是什么让一名天才飞行士，一个明明热爱飞行的人变成了这样？
t_en: What turned an ace pilot — someone who loved to fly — into someone like her?

t_ch: 母亲将这件圣物留在身上，显然还怀念着自己的飞行生涯。可她却不肯再飞行了，甚至不让我成为斗舰飞行士，到底是为什么呢？
t_en: My mom clearly misses her flying days, otherwise she wouldn't keep this on her person. But she doesn't fly anymore and refuses to let me become a fighter pilot. Why?

t_ch: 也许我们可以旁敲侧击，问问知情人士。
t_en: Maybe we can ask some people who might know the truth.

t_ch: 去问问景元？
t_en: Like Jing Yuan?

t_ch: 嗯…神策府将军公务繁忙，我们未必见得到他，但有试一试的价值。驭空司舵是他一手提拔上来的，那些往事他大概比谁都清楚。
t_en: Well... the general's been busy at the Seat of Divine Foresight recently — we might not get to see him right away. Still, it's worth a try — he was the one who promoted Helm Master Yukong — he probably knows more about her past than anyone.

t_ch: 去天舶司问问？
t_en: We could ask around the Sky-Faring Commission?

t_ch: 如果是司舵本人不愿提及的事情，天舶司的人们即使知情，恐怕也不愿轻易说出口吧。我倒是觉得，可以先去问问景元将军。
t_en: If the helm master herself is reluctant to talk about it, then the Sky-Faring Commission staff would be even less likely — even if they know the truth. I think we should try General Jing Yuan first.

t_ch: 驭空司舵是他一手提拔上来的，那些往事他大概比谁都清楚。
t_en: He promoted Helm Master Yukong himself — he probably knows more about her past than anyone.

t_ch: 谁是知情人士？
t_en: Who would that be?

t_ch: 不如先去问问景元将军吧。神策府将军公务繁忙，我们未必见得到他，但有试一试的价值。驭空司舵是他一手提拔上来的，那些往事他大概比谁都清楚。
t_en: I think we should try General Jing Yuan first. He's been busy at the Seat of Divine Foresight recently — we might not get to see him right away. Still, it's worth a try — he was the one who promoted Helm Master Yukong — he probably knows more about her past than anyone.

t_ch: 景元将军？直接去问他合适吗…哦，也对，二位本来就是将军的贵客…但即使如此，这也太给二位添麻烦了……
t_en: General Jing Yuan? Are you sure it's okay to go straight to him? ...Oh, right, you're the general's guests... Even so, this is asking a lot of you...

t_ch: 没关系。如果能弄清楚驭空司舵不再愿意飞行的原因，大概也就能弄清楚她不愿意让晴霓小姐成为斗舰飞行士的原因了。
t_en: No problem. If we can get to the bottom of why Helm Master Yukong no longer flies, we might be able to find out why she doesn't want you to be a fighter pilot.

t_ch: 虽然在这件事上，我们没办法直接帮到你，但也许能间接帮你找出一道说服令堂的突破口。
t_en: We can't help you too directly, but an indirect conversation like this might give us a clue on how to convince your mother.

t_ch: 交给我们吧！
t_en: Leave it to us!

t_ch: 嗯，交给我们吧。
t_en: Yes, leave it to us.

t_ch: 感觉和景元也没那么熟…
t_en: Do we really know Jing Yuan that well?

t_ch: 没关系，景元将军是个心胸宽广的人。我们为了一个年轻人的未来去叨扰他，他大概也不会怪罪。
t_en: Don't worry — General Jing Yuan is understanding. I'm sure a small visit won't trouble him — especially if it's about a young girl's future.

t_ch: 那就…有劳二位恩公了…我再去星槎海随便逛逛，暂时还不是很想回到天舶司……
t_en: In that case... thank you so much, benefactors... I'll head back to Starskiff Haven. I'm not ready to return to the Sky-Faring Commission yet...

t_ch: 瓦尔特先生，还有{NICKNAME}？二位这次来神策府，是有什么事情要办吗？
t_en: Mr. Yang, and {NICKNAME}? What brings you to the Seat of Divine Foresight this time?

t_ch: 并不是什么特别要紧的事，只是…想了解一下驭空司舵。
t_en: It's nothing urgent. We were hoping to... understand Helm Master Yukong a little better.

t_ch: 驭空的事？我听说了，天舶司中有位文员开星槎抢险，结果自己遇到了危险，幸有两位异邦人仗义搭救，才保全了性命。我要代表罗浮感谢你们。
t_en: Yukong? I heard an office worker from the Sky-Faring Commission flew a starskiff to assist in the present crisis, but ran into trouble herself. She was only saved thanks to a couple of travelers... On behalf of the Luofu, you have my thanks, once again.

t_ch: 驭空少见地大发雷霆，连我神策府这厢都能听到些动静。所以二位这是卷入了那对母女之间的麻烦事了？
t_en: The Seat of Divine Foresight heard that Yukong's seldom-seen but often-feared temper was on display. I believe the two of you were caught between mother and daughter?

t_ch: 我听说驭空司舵曾是个了不起的飞行士，但却拒绝了她女儿步上同样的道路……
t_en: ...We heard that Helm Master Yukong was an outstanding pilot, but that she refuses to let her daughter embark on the same career...

t_ch: 这样的问题，只有当事人亲口回答才有意义。
t_en: Yukong herself is best placed to answer such questions...

t_ch: 当然了，你们来此也是因为驭空司舵对这么做的理由绝口不提。
t_en: ...but naturally, you are here because you fear that Helm Master Yukong would decline to answer.

t_ch: 如果是为了解答晴霓小姐的好奇心，我们不妨将话题放得更遥远一些？对于身为飞行士的驭空，你们想了解什么呢？
t_en: If you wish to dispel Miss Qingni's curiosity, perhaps we can take a small step back from the topic at hand. What do you wish to know about Yukong the pilot?

t_ch: 这是…帝弓司命的圣物？诸位从何处得到这东西的？
t_en: This is... a divine object from the Reignbow Arbiter? Where did you find this?

t_ch: 在驭空随身的挂饰里。
t_en: Inside a trinket belonging to Yukong.

t_ch: 这件圣物一直存放在驭空司舵随身携带的挂饰中，晴霓小姐暂时交给了我们。
t_en: This object has been stored inside Helm Master Yukong's personal trinket all along — Miss Qingni lent it to us.

t_ch: 晴霓给我们的。
t_en: Qingni gave it to us.

t_ch: 这件圣物一直存放在驭空司舵随身携带的挂饰中，晴霓小姐暂时交给了我们。
t_en: This object has been stored inside Helm Master Yukong's personal trinket all along — Miss Qingni lent it to us.

t_ch: …路上捡来的。
t_en: ...Picked it up off the road.

t_ch: 哪条路上捡来的？下次我也去找找。这种品相的圣物，在市面上至少值两万巡镝。
t_en: Which road? I should head there myself — a divine object like this possesses a market value of at least <unbreak>20,000</unbreak> strales.

t_ch: {F#她}{M#他}开玩笑的。这件圣物一直存放在驭空司舵随身携带的挂饰中，晴霓小姐暂时交给了我们。
t_en: ...{F#She's}{M#He's} kidding. This object has been stored inside Helm Master Yukong's personal trinket all along — Miss Qingni lent it to us.

t_ch: 她想借着这件东西，打听驭空大人当年的经历，还有…她不再飞行的理由。
t_en: She thought it might be a way to find out more about Madam Yukong's past, and... why she no longer flies.

t_ch: 这件圣物并不是普通的工艺品，它可算得上是铭记血泪的见证。
t_en: This object is no ordinary trinket. It has borne witness to blood and tears.

t_ch: 诸位对仙舟巡猎星海之事耳熟能详。但一定甚少听闻「丰饶民战争」。在仙舟清除不死孽物的同时，丰饶孽物的联军也多次突破战线，将仙舟逼入危境。
t_en: I'm sure you're both familiar with the Xianzhou's long galactic hunt, but have you heard of the Denizens of Abundance Wars? As the Xianzhou has continued to cleanse the universe of immortal abominations, so too has the Abundance Axis succeeded in breaching our defensive lines. They pushed the Xianzhou to the brink of disaster.

t_ch: 三十年前，曜青与方壶两座仙舟受到敌人的围攻。即使在联盟与丰饶之民漫长的战争史中，那场空战也称得上是最为惨烈的战役之一。
t_en: Thirty years ago, the Xianzhou Yaoqing and Fanghu were besieged by our enemies. Even in the long history of warfare between the Xianzhou Alliance and the Denizens of Abundance, that air battle was one of the most tragic.

t_ch: 面对数倍于自己的丰饶孽物，近百万斗舰飞行士殊死搏斗，活着离开战场的，不足十万。
t_en: We were greatly outnumbered by the Abominations of Abundance. Almost a million fighter pilots fought in the bitter battle. Those who survived numbered no more than a hundred thousand.

t_ch: 而在这场失利战役的尾声，若不是帝弓司命示现降临，用倾天光矢摧毁了丰饶孽物的攻势，罗浮今日是否还能继续航行，也难说得很。
t_en: Had the Reignbow Arbiter not descended and destroyed the enemy's assault with their Sky-Shattering Lux Arrow, who's to say whether the Luofu would still be here today?

t_ch: 铸成这些圣物的材料，便是帝弓神矢在战场上留下的「余烬」。对那些幸存者来说，其中掺杂着故友的鲜血，敌人的灰烬，还有自己的回忆……
t_en: Such divine objects were forged from the embers that the Arbiter's divine arrow left behind. For the survivors, these relics contain the blood of their comrades, the ashes of their enemies, and the dust of their memories...

t_ch: 驭空，就是这场战役的幸存者。而她最好的朋友采翼，埋骨异乡，再也没能回来。
t_en: Yukong is a survivor of that war. Her best friend, Caiyi, perished on the battlefield.

t_ch: 晴霓告诉了你们这些吗？
t_en: Did Qingni tell you any of this?

t_ch: 真怀念啊，当年那个被云骑军控诉超速飞行，抓到我面前的狐人小姑娘，现在已经是和我平起平坐的司舵大人了。
t_en: Hehe, it all serves to remind me... A long time ago, a young foxian girl, charged by the Knights with a high-speed flight violation, was brought before me as punishment... now she's the Madam Helm Master — my equal.

t_ch: 如果我告诉当年的自己：「这个小姑娘以后会成为老成持重的司舵。」恐怕自己怎么也不会信的。
t_en: If I were to turn back the sands of time and try to convince my past self that the little girl in front of me would one day become a wise and experienced helm master... I would probably never have believed it.

t_ch: 当年的驭空绝不是如今这般模样，案牍劳形，枯坐书斋。
t_en: The Yukong of the past was nothing like she is now — spending her days surrounded by documents at her desk.

t_ch: 她的性子像烈火，像停不下来的风。要说谁能稍稍让她安静片刻…只有那位战友了。
t_en: She was a flaming wind. The only one who could ever calm her... was her comrade.

t_ch: 战友？
t_en: Comrade?

t_ch: 是啊，体积较大的斗舰，需要飞行士两两成对合作。一人掌舵飞行，另一人操控武器。
t_en: Yes. Large fighter starskiffs need to be operated in pairs. One pilot takes the helm, the other controls the turrets.

t_ch: 王牌飞行士的传奇，也需要王牌武备士来成全。和驭空搭档的那个女孩名叫采翼。
t_en: The legend of the ace fighter pilot is incomplete without the ace gunner. Yukong's partner was Caiyi.

t_ch: 无论是驱驾斗舰，还是执掌火力，采翼都像一把冰刀，干脆利落，毫无多余的情绪。
t_en: Both at the controls and behind the turret — Caiyi was like a cold blade... efficient, decisive, unfeeling when she had to be.

t_ch: 真难想象，性格这样极端的两个人竟然能合作无间。如果没有那场重创了仙舟空军的战役的话……
t_en: It's hard to imagine such contrasting personalities working in perfect harmony... As I mentioned, the campaign inflicted heavy losses on the Xianzhou air force...

t_ch: 怎么？还有什么事吗？
t_en: Oh? Something else on your mind?

t_ch: 还有一些问题…
t_en: I have a few other questions...

t_ch: 没事了。
t_en: Nothing.

t_ch: 我没有其他问题了，多谢将军。
t_en: I don't have any other questions. Thank you, general.

t_ch: 驭空司舵不愿再飞行的心结，我不便揣测，也不想撕开她的旧伤。
t_en: I do not wish to speculate on the reason behind Helm Master Yukong's reluctance to fly, nor do I want to open up old wounds.

t_ch: 没有人能轻易抛下自己的过去。驭空和采翼是出生入死的挚友，她一定留有关于两人过去的一些回忆。
t_en: However, it's difficult to discard one's past. Yukong and Caiyi trusted each other with their lives. The helm master will have strong memories of that time.

t_ch: 这话本不该由我说出口，但对晴霓来说，她有权知道过去的事情，因为这些过去开始左右她的未来了。
t_en: Perhaps it is not my place to say, but I believe Qingni has the right to know about the past... as the past is starting to affect her future.

t_ch: 多谢了，将军。
t_en: Thank you, general.

t_ch: 有劳将军，用这样的琐事占用你的时间，真是抱歉。
t_en: Thank you, general. We apologize for taking up your time with such triviality.

t_ch: 瓦尔特先生说笑了，这是关乎这对母女互相理解的事情，又怎么能算是小事呢？要我说，神策府里的文山会海才是小事。
t_en: Mr. Yang, you jest. How can harmony between parent and child constitute a triviality? If you ask me, the mountains of documents in the Seat of Divine Foresight are the only triviality around here.

t_ch: 那么，我们就先告辞了。
t_en: Mm, we'll take our leave.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，咱们走吧。去星槎海找找晴霓。
t_en: Let's go, {NICKNAME}. We should head to Starskiff Haven and find Qingni.

t_ch: 你们来啦。我妈好像…心情不太好，去外面散心了。
t_en: You're here. My mom... isn't in a great mood. She went for a walk.

t_ch: 心情不太好？
t_en: Isn't in a great mood?

t_ch: 嗯…应该是因为我吧……
t_en: Mm-hmm... probably because of me...

t_ch: 翘班了？
t_en: She skipped work?

t_ch: 我妈可不是不负责任的人。她肯定是把工作安排妥帖后才去翘班的。
t_en: Hehe, my mom isn't the irresponsible type — she only skips work if she's managed to delegate everything.

t_ch: 你们母女可真像啊。
t_en: You two are very similar.

t_ch: 是啊，每次吵完架，我们都会跑出去散心。只不过，我去星槎海的一边，她去星槎海的另一边…这样可以避免吵完架后立刻见面。
t_en: Well, we do both like to take a walk after a fight. The only difference is that I come to this side of Starskiff Haven, and she heads to the other... That way we don't bump into each other.

t_ch: 有点可爱…
t_en: It's actually kind of cute...

t_ch: 真的吗？我倒是觉得挺傻的。
t_en: Really? I think it's silly.

t_ch: 有点幼稚…
t_en: That's a bit childish...

t_ch: 是啊，是很幼稚。
t_en: Yeah, I agree.

t_ch: 有点傻…
t_en: That's a bit silly...

t_ch: 是啊，是挺傻的。
t_en: You can say that again.

t_ch: 说回正题…二位从景元将军那里知道了些什么吗？
t_en: Anyway... did you find anything out from General Jing Yuan?

t_ch: 向晴霓转述了景元提供的信息。
t_en: You relay the information provided by Jing Yuan to Qingni.

t_ch: 驭空司舵从没向你提起过这些吗？
t_en: Did Helm Master Yukong never bring this up with you?

t_ch: 我很想知道关于她的事，但她总是扯开话题，要不就是装作没听见。
t_en: *sigh* I was always curious about her past, but she either dodged my questions or pretended not to hear them.

t_ch: 我大概也能猜到，妈妈过去经历的某些事，是一道永远无法愈合的伤疤。所以，在懂事之后，我就再也没有问出口。
t_en: In the end, I realized she probably experienced something that left a scar... a scar that never fully healed. I never asked her again.

t_ch: 她害怕你重蹈挚友的覆辙…
t_en: She was afraid of you ending up like her friend...

t_ch: 是啊，驭空自此之后不愿飞行，也不愿你飞行的原因，恐怕正在于此。
t_en: Indeed. That's probably why Yukong was never willing to fly again after the battle, and why she forbids you from flying.

t_ch: 天才也有幸运和不幸之分…
t_en: Even a flying ace can get into trouble...

t_ch: 是啊，驭空并不想你背负她的虚名。何况在生死战场上，人的天赋和名声其实并无意义。
t_en: Yes, Yukong doesn't want you to feel the pressure of her name. Besides, reputation and talent count for something, but you need luck on the battlefield.

t_ch: 你是说，她想起自己失去挚友的伤痛，所以才不肯让我飞行。我越有天赋，她就越是害怕，害怕我重蹈覆辙？
t_en: So, she doesn't want me to fly because she remembers the pain of losing her friend... and she thinks that my talent makes it more likely that she'll lose me in the same way?

t_ch: 我不敢妄言令堂的想法，晴霓小姐还是应该亲自和她谈谈。
t_en: I can't guarantee that's what she's thinking, Miss Qingni. You should talk with her yourself.

t_ch: 嗯…瓦尔特先生说得对。在此之前，我还有些事要确认。景元将军所说的「过去」，应该就存放在司辰宫的某个档案柜里。
t_en: Hmm... You're right, Mr. Yang... but I still have something else to check first. This past that General Jing Yuan mentioned is probably archived in the Palace of Astrum.

t_ch: 我曾经看到过她将某些东西珍而重之地放入案牍库归档，唔，像是日记之类的文件。
t_en: I remember watching her handle a document with great care before she filed it away... it looked like a diary.

t_ch: 晴霓小姐还记得这份日记被存放在哪里吗？
t_en: Do you still remember where it was filed, Miss Qingni?

t_ch: 隐约有些印象。但天舶司归档内容众多，能不能请二位恩公和我一起找找看……
t_en: Vaguely. But there are so many files stored in the Sky-Faring Commission. Could I ask you benefactors to look for it with me...?

t_ch: 作为外客翻找天舶司的档案…实在有失礼貌。晴霓小姐，我们只能负责陪同，查找的事情还请你自己动手。
t_en: Hmm... A couple of outworlders going through Sky-Faring Commission files isn't a good look... I'm afraid we can only accompany you, Miss Qingni — you'll need to do the searching yourself.

t_ch: 是我欠考虑了。
t_en: Of course, you're right, sorry!

t_ch: 这台术算机正在运行一个航路模拟系统，系统显示还需要两个时辰，就能模拟出罗浮到科德莱浮游城的航路。
t_en: This calculation device is running a voyage simulation. It shows that it needs two more dual-hours to simulate the voyage from the Luofu to the floating city of Cordelia.

t_ch: 这上面看起来正在运行很重要的程序。还是别乱动这台术算机了。
t_en: It seems like this device is handling some important procedures. Let's leave it alone.

t_ch: 这台术算机的桌面图片是一行飘逸的书法字：「岩明工位，闲人勿动。」
t_en: The desktop background on this calculation device is a line of floating calligraphy — "Yanming's work station, unauthorized personnel prohibited."

t_ch: 岩明先生一板一眼的，可不好惹，咱们还是换一台术算机好了。
t_en: Mr. Yanming is a stickler for rules — I don't wanna annoy him. Let's go to a different device.

t_ch: 这台术算机不停跳动着字符和日期。
t_en: This calculation device is continuously cycling through symbols and dates.

t_ch: 这台术算机是不是坏掉了？
t_en: Is this one broken?

t_ch: 不是…这是夕葵小姐的工作日程…她可真是个大忙人。
t_en: No... this is Miss Xikui's daily schedule... she's super busy.

s_ch: 正在摸鱼的天舶司职员
t_ch: 呃…你们在看什么？我可没在摸鱼，我在用帝垣琼玉训练术算机的运算能力…我…我这可是正经事！绝对不是在打牌！
t_en: Uhh... can I help you? I—I know what it looks like, but I'm using Celestial Jade to... improve the algorithm! I—I'm working, I swear!

t_ch: …抱歉，打扰了。
t_en: Uh sorry... Sorry, we'll get going.

s_ch: 正在释放怨念的天舶司职员
t_ch: 我爱工作。我爱加班。我爱工作。我爱加班。我爱工作。我爱加班。我爱工作。我爱加班。
t_en: I love work. I love overtime. I love work. I love overtime. I love work. I love overtime. I love work. I love overtime.

t_ch: 呃…还是不要打扰她了……
t_en: Umm... let's leave her alone...

t_ch: 这台确实可以用！好的…唔…我查查，历代司舵的私人档案归档于…找到了。我们过去看看吧。
t_en: We can use this one! Okay... hmm... lemme see — helm masters, personal archive... got it! Let's go take a look.

t_ch: 应该就是这里了。我看看…找到了！这就是妈妈的日记。
t_en: It should be here. Let's see... yes! This is Mom's diary.

t_ch: 晴霓小姐，这是令堂的私人日记，我想我们不该看的。
t_en: Miss Qingni, I don't think the two of us should be reading your mother's diary.

t_ch: 其实没关系吧…都是几十年前的东西了，对你们异邦人来说，大半辈子都过去了。
t_en: It should be fine — this is from decades ago. For you travelers, that's more than half a lifespan.

t_ch: 不，我还是觉得…我不该看。{NICKNAME}，你在这里陪着晴霓小姐吧，我在旁边等你们。
t_en: No, I still think... I shouldn't read it. {NICKNAME}, you stay here with Miss Qingni. I'll be waiting over there.

t_ch: 哎…好吧。
t_en: *sigh* ...Fine.

t_ch: 其实真的没什么。我印象里，就只是一些对飞行士生活的感慨。
t_en: Is it really that big of a deal? I'm sure they're just her notes on life as a pilot...

t_ch: 杨叔比较讲究。
t_en: Mr. Yang has rules for these things.

t_ch: 完全理解。瓦尔特先生一看就是这样的人，有一套坚定的原则，并且绝不会越线。
t_en: I understand, I can tell Mr. Yang is a principled person — there are some lines he won't cross.

t_ch: 我也不该看…
t_en: I shouldn't look either...

t_ch: 别啊，算我求您了，和我一起看吧。总要有个人帮我出出主意吧。
t_en: C'mon, pleeeease look at it with me? I need someone to give me some advice.

t_ch: 咱们一起读一读吧，也许能从日记里找到些什么。
t_en: Let's read it together. Maybe her diary holds the key to all of this.

t_ch: 你们干什么呢？司舵的宝贝闺女带着个外人在这里东摸摸西摸摸，像什么样子？
t_en: What is the meaning of this? The Helm Master's daughter, bringing outworlders in to sneak around...?

t_ch: 你们手里拿的是什么？我看看…驭空大人的…日记？！好大的胆子啊！
t_en: What do you have in your hands? Let me see... *gasp* Madam Yukong's diary!? The nerve!

t_ch: 驭空大人的私人日记你也敢翻？还带着人家的闺女一起翻？帝弓在上，你们这可真是……
t_en: How dare you read Madam Yukong's personal diary! And with her daughter, too!? By the Arbiter, you two are in for a—

t_ch: 岩明先生，您先消消气…我们这么做是有原因的……
t_en: Mr. Yanming, please, calm down — let us explain...

t_ch: 晴霓将事情的来龙去脉讲给了岩明。
t_en: Qingni tells Yanming the whole story.

t_ch: 事情的经过我了解了…晴霓啊，驭空大人不让你做飞行士，我知道你心有不甘，可她也有她的顾虑……
t_en: I see... Qingni, I know Madam Yukong's position on you becoming a pilot is tough for you to accept, but she has her own concerns...

t_ch: 岩明先生，您说的我都明白。但是事到如今，比起自己能否成为斗舰飞行士，我更想知道妈妈经历了什么……
t_en: I understand, Mr. Yanming... but now, compared to becoming a fighter pilot, I'm more interested in knowing what my Mom went through...

t_ch: 我想知道是什么将她从那位天才飞行士，变成了现在的驭空司舵。
t_en: I want to know what happened... how she went from ace pilot to helm master.

t_ch: 我在这里也有些年月了，有什么想知道的，你们不妨直接问我吧…别到处乱翻了，这习惯不好。
t_en: *sigh* I've been here for a long time, Qingni. If there's something you wish to know, just ask... but don't go rummaging around — that's a bad habit.

t_ch: 采翼小姐吗…我记得她。采翼可是当时数一数二的武备士，想和她同飞的人数不胜数。
t_en: Miss Caiyi? ...I remember her. She was an ace gunner — many people wanted to be her flight partner.

t_ch: 她的丈夫，广渊先生则是个飞行士。夫妻共同驾驶斗舰，真是令人羡慕得紧。
t_en: Her husband, Mr. Guangyuan, was a pilot. They manned the same skiff, and were the envy of many...

t_ch: 不过要说和她最合拍，也是最常搭档的，还得是你母亲。她们二人出击的次数，连广渊先生也屈居其后。尤其是在广渊先生阵亡后，采翼几乎不和你母亲以外的人搭档。
t_en: ...but when it came to pilots, your mother was the best match for her — they flew together all the time. Even Mr. Guangyuan couldn't match their numbers. After he passed away in battle, Caiyi rarely teamed up with anyone except your mother.

t_ch: 唉，可惜，没过多久采翼也不在了。飞行士的人生便是这样，没人知道这一次飞行会不会是最后一次。
t_en: *sigh* It was tragic when Caiyi died soon after. That's the life of a pilot — no one knows which flight will be their last.

t_ch: 采翼的死，对驭空大人的打击很大。那场战役后，她没有按惯例那样上交采翼的遗物，而是留在自己身边，时不时拿出来翻看。
t_en: Caiyi's death was a big blow to Madam Yukong. After the battle, she refused to turn in Caiyi's possessions as per regulations. She kept them locked away — occasionally taking them out to admire.

t_ch: 没想到岩明先生还挺八卦的…
t_en: I didn't think you'd know this many details, Mr. Yanming...

t_ch: 什么话。作为仙舟人，我唯一的长处就是活得够久，能见证的东西要比那些朝生暮死的狐人飞行士更多。
t_en: Nonsense. As a Xianzhou native, I've lived long enough to remember more than the foxian pilots could ever hope to.

t_ch: 唉，到头来，那些了不起的天才都走啦。我唯一能做的也就是回忆回忆，让别人不要那么快忘了她们。
t_en: In the end, all the ace pilots perished. The only thing I can do is to remember them and ensure they are not forgotten too soon.

t_ch: 你怎么那么关注驭空啊？
t_en: Why do you pay so much attention to Yukong?

t_ch: 呃，那个…作为下属关心上级的心理健康有这么难理解吗？
t_en: Well, I... As her subordinate, surely I should be concerned with her mental well-being?

t_ch: 她要是不高兴，我能快活得起来吗？我是说，她要是不高兴，我的职场生涯能快活得起来吗？
t_en: If Madam Yukong is unhappy, how can I be happy? I mean to say, how can I be happy in my duties to her?

t_ch: 采翼小姐的遗物，究竟放在了哪里呢？
t_en: Where are Miss Caiyi's possessions stored?

t_ch: 这件圣物…如果我没记错，是驭空大人一直随身携带的那件吧？
t_en: This divine object... If I remember correctly, it's the one Madam Yukong always carries with her?

t_ch: 我听说过，那场战役能安然结束，全都仰赖帝弓的庇佑。如果驭空大人始终还带着那枚飞行士的挂件，那么也许她其实也一直都眷恋着天空……
t_en: I heard the war that forged this object was only won thanks to the Reignbow Arbiter's blessing. If Madam Yukong still carries objects from that pilot, then perhaps she still longs for the sky...

t_ch: 如果妈妈还眷恋天空，她又为什么不再飞行了呢？
t_en: If Mom still longs for the sky, then why does she no longer fly?

t_ch: 可能是因为采翼的离去，对她的打击太过沉重。这事儿你也不该问我，问你妈妈去。
t_en: Perhaps Caiyi's passing was too much for her. You shouldn't be asking me about this — ask your mother.

t_ch: 不。这不是我该看的东西，别拿给我看……
t_en: No, this isn't something I should read. Don't show it to me...

t_ch: 看看又不会少块肉。
t_en: C'mon, we've both had a look.

t_ch: 瞧你这话说的…吃鞋垫子还不会少块肉呢，你吃吗？
t_en: Just listen to yourself... I suppose you think if you ate your shoes I'd follow suit?

t_ch: 还是问点别的吧，问点我能回答的。
t_en: Just ask some other questions. Ones I can answer.

t_ch: 那算了，我们聊点别的。
t_en: Never mind. Let's talk about something else.

t_ch: 嗯…问点别的吧，问点我能回答的。
t_en: Yes... Just ask some other questions. Ones I can answer.

t_ch: 驭空不会知道的。
t_en: Yukong won't know.

t_ch: 她确实不会知道，但是君子要慎独。
t_en: She won't, but I have my principles.

t_ch: 还是问点别的吧，问点我能回答的。
t_en: Just ask some other questions. Ones I can answer.

t_ch: 无论如何，谢谢你告诉了我们这些，岩明先生。
t_en: Thanks for telling us all this, Mr. Yanming.

t_ch: 没关系…这毕竟是对你而言相当重要的事……
t_en: You're welcome... I know this is important to you...

t_ch: 我知道，接下来你们肯定要去翻驭空大人的办公桌了。
t_en: ...I suppose you're going to go through Madam Yukong's office desk next?

t_ch: 晴霓，我其实很明白你的心情。我会当做什么都不知道地离开——在领导隐私曝光的时刻不在场，这也是咱职场人最基本的素养了。
t_en: ...I can understand your position, Qingni. I'll take my leave and pretend I didn't see anything. After all, it's an unwritten rule that subordinates should be absent when their superior's privacy is exposed.

t_ch: 你可千万别跟你妈提起今天见过我！
t_en: Please, don't tell your mother that you saw me today!

t_ch: 我懂。
t_en: Got it.

t_ch: 谢谢岩明先生！
t_en: Thank you, Mr. Yanming!

t_ch: 你是天舶司保安？
t_en: So much for Sky-Faring Commission security.

t_ch: 保护公共财产和公共秩序，这是每个仙舟人民应尽的责任。
t_en: Ensuring the security of public assets and order is the duty of every Xianzhou citizen.

t_ch: 算你识相。
t_en: Don't let us catch you nosying around here again!

t_ch: 倒霉孩子，我那是懒得跟你计较。
t_en: Hehe, you're a cheeky one... You're lucky I have a sense of humor.

t_ch: 你们赶紧去吧。过一会儿，驭空大人就该回来了。
t_en: Hurry. Madam Yukong will be back soon.

t_ch: 嗯？你们还有什么事吗？驭空大人可快要回来了啊。
t_en: Mm? Do you have any other questions? Madam Yukong will be back soon.

t_ch: 还有些事想问…
t_en: I have other questions...

t_ch: 没事了。
t_en: Nothing important.

t_ch: 我妈在办公桌里放了很多东西，咱们得找找。
t_en: My mom keeps lots of stuff in her desk. Let's have a look.

t_ch: 这桌子里哪有地方放东西？
t_en: This desk has space to put stuff?

t_ch: 暗格藏在哪儿了…
t_en: There must be a hidden compartment...

t_ch: 这就是仙舟科技吗…
t_en: Is this some special Xianzhou technology...?

t_ch: 这款办公桌的卖点就是这样啊。极简的设计配合超大容量的暗格，很多文职人员都用这一款。
t_en: That's the selling point of this desk: Super simplistic design with super spacious hidden compartments. Lots of office staff use this desk.

t_ch: 桌子的左边、右边和桌面下边各有一个暗格，要先看哪个呢？
t_en: There are hidden compartments on the left, right, and under the desktop. Which one should we check first?

t_ch: 左边。
t_en: Left side.

t_ch: 纸鸢…一些私人书信…还有一本《厌作人间语》。原来妈妈平时喜欢这种小说，还以为她会看更加「豪迈」的东西。
t_en: A paper kite... personal letters... and a copy of Otherworldly Delights? So Mom likes these kinds of novels? I thought she'd prefer more heroic stuff...

t_ch: 不过我们要找的东西似乎不在这里。
t_en: ...but what we're looking for isn't here.

t_ch: 右边。
t_en: Right side.

t_ch: 里面有个展阅诶，搞不好是我们要找的东西。啊！这是…阵亡将士的档案…妈妈将他们每个人的信息，放在自己手边。
t_en: There's some exhibition material, not sure if it's relevant... Ah! This is... the fallen heroes archive... Mom stored them right next to her..

t_ch: 从小到大，每次我们的飞行士从战场上凯旋，母亲总会提醒我：「永远不要忘记胜利的代价。」
t_en: Every time our pilots returned from the battlefield, my mother would tell me never to forget the cost of victory...

t_ch: 不过我们要找的东西似乎不在这里。
t_en: ...but what we're looking for isn't here.

t_ch: 下边。
t_en: Under the desktop.

t_ch: …找到了。
t_en: ...Got it.

t_ch: 这本册子倒是和妈妈那本日记形制很像。不过看起来，应该是采翼小姐的日记……
t_en: This looks similar to Mom's diary... but it looks like it belonged to Miss Caiyi...

t_ch: 所以，我是被妈妈收养的孩子？而这位采翼小姐…才是我的母亲？
t_en: So... I was adopted? And this Miss Caiyi... was my real mother?

t_ch: 虽然内心隐隐约约预感，但真的得知这些，我还是有种像是在做梦一样的感觉。
t_en: ...I suppose I've always had a feeling... but now I know the truth, I feel like I'm dreaming...

t_ch: 「别让那孩子触碰天空…」
t_en: "Don't let that child touch the sky..."

t_ch: 这就是妈妈拒绝让我成为斗舰飞行士的理由吗？
t_en: Is this why Mom refuses to let me become a fighter pilot?

t_ch: 其实我早猜到了…
t_en: I kinda guessed this myself...

t_ch: 难怪小时候我问妈妈，爸爸在哪儿时，她回答我，我是从迴星港的星槎流水线上种出来的……
t_en: When I was little, I asked my Mom where my dad was... She said I was grown from the starskiff assembly line at Stargazer Navalia...

t_ch: 我当时想，骗小孩子都这么不上心，还疑心她是夸我继承了她的天赋。
t_en: I thought she was just a bad liar... I even thought she meant I'd inherited her talent with starskiffs.

t_ch: 她不想失去另一个采翼…
t_en: She doesn't want to lose Caiyi again...

t_ch: 对于飞行士来说，载回同伴，是起飞前最重要的誓言。
t_en: For a pilot, the most important oath before departure is the promise to bring your comrade back.

t_ch: 妈妈没能载回同伴，她只能遵守这个誓言，不让另一个采翼出现。对她来说，我不仅仅是她的女儿，也是她能为采翼履行的最后责任。
t_en: Mom didn't manage to bring her comrade back... so she vowed to prevent me from setting out. Not only am I her daughter — I'm also her final promise to Caiyi.

t_ch: 我…我从没想过自己的梦想会让她如此为难。
t_en: I... I never thought my dream would put Mom in such a difficult situation.

t_ch: 不知为什么，翻完这册日记，我突然觉得和她亲近了很多。虽然意外知道了我们不是血脉相连的母女，但我比过去更了解她了。
t_en: ...I feel a lot closer to Mom after reading this diary. Even if we're not connected by blood, I understand her a lot more now.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，能请您再陪我一次吗，我想和妈妈好好谈谈。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, can you come with me one more time? I want to have a proper chat with Mom.

t_ch: 没问题。
t_en: No problem.

t_ch: 太好了，谢谢您！
t_en: Yes! Thank you!

t_ch: 可这是你的家事…
t_en: But this is a family affair...

t_ch: 没关系…我也并不需要您帮我做什么…只希望您能和瓦尔特先生站在一旁，替我壮壮胆吧……
t_en: It's fine, I don't need you to do anything... I just want you and Mr. Yang to stand nearby... I'll feel more confident that way...

t_ch: 真能说服她吗？
t_en: Can we really convince her?

t_ch: 我想做最后一次努力。如果即使这样，妈妈都不同意的话…那我也只好甘愿做一个天舶司的文员了。
t_en: I want to try one last time. If Mom still doesn't agree... then I'll resign myself to signing documents in the Sky-Faring Commission.

t_ch: 事情都办完了？怎么样，还顺利吗？
t_en: You're all done? Did it go smoothly?

t_ch: 你给瓦尔特转述了刚才发生的事。
t_en: You tell Welt everything that just happened.

t_ch: …明白了。那我们陪你去找驭空司舵吧……
t_en: ...I see. Let's find Helm Master Yukong — we'll accompany you...

t_ch: 不过…虽然我们都很希望能帮到你，但在与令堂交涉时，于情于理，我和{NICKNAME}都是不能直接插手的。
t_en: ...We're here to help, but {NICKNAME} and I really shouldn't interject when you're conversing with your mother.

t_ch: 嗯，没关系，我完全理解。况且二位恩公就算只是在我身边站着，就已经可以给我莫大的勇气了。
t_en: Mm, it's okay, I understand. Just having you stand next to me is already a great help, benefactors.

t_ch: 妈妈心情不好时，就会去一个能看到玉界门的地方散心。她现在应该还在那里，我们过去找她吧。
t_en: When Mom feels down, she usually goes to take in a view of the Jade Gate. She should still be there, let's go find her.

t_ch: 妈妈……
t_en: Mom...

t_ch: 晴霓？怎么瓦尔特先生和{NICKNAME}也在这里？
t_en: Qingni? Why are Mr. Yang and {NICKNAME} here too?

t_ch: 晴霓小姐为人热情，带我们去星槎海游览了一番。在路上听说她想和驭空司舵重归于好，又有些羞怯，于是便冒昧地陪她一起来了。
t_en: Miss Qingni kindly agreed to show us around Starskiff Haven. She mentioned she was trying to mend things between you, but was feeling shy about it. We volunteered to come with her.

t_ch: 小女为人幼稚，让二位多费心了。
t_en: Thank you. I must apologize for the trouble my daughter has brought you.

t_ch: 妈！
t_en: Mom!

t_ch: 行了，天色也不早了，有什么话，回家再说吧。
t_en: Alright, it's getting late. Let's talk once we're home.

t_ch: 其实…我有事情想和您谈谈。
t_en: Actually... I want to talk to you about something right now.

t_ch: 无论如何，我都想成为斗舰飞行士。
t_en: I want to become a fighter pilot, no matter what.

t_ch: 这件事，我们已经谈过很多次了。不行就是不行。
t_en: *sigh* We've talked about this many times. No means no.

t_ch: 可是…可是……
t_en: But...

t_ch: 晴霓小姐似乎有些犹豫。这种时候，就需要你这样的同辈人推她一把了。
t_en: Seems like Miss Qingni is a little hesitant. Try giving her a little push.

t_ch: （说是不该插手，但杨叔还是按捺不住啊…那么该帮晴霓提起哪件事呢？）
t_en: (I thought Mr. Yang said we shouldn't interject... What should I say?)

t_ch: （这件圣物似乎可以证明驭空对「巡猎」的虔信…晴霓认为这代表驭空对斗舰飞行士的生涯尚怀眷恋。）
t_en: (This divine object is a testament to Yukong's devotion to The Hunt... Qingni believes it's a sign that Yukong still misses the life of a fighter pilot...)

t_ch: （但这似乎不是现在该提出的话题。）
t_en: (...Still, it doesn't seem like something to bring up now.)

t_ch: （在晴霓的回忆中，驭空似乎一直对斗舰飞行士的生涯讳莫如深，也不希望晴霓和自己走上同样的道路…这是为什么呢？）
t_en: (Qingni remembers that Yukong has always avoided talking about her career as a fighter pilot, and that she wants to prevent Qingni from following the same path... Why?)

t_ch: （但这似乎不是现在该提出的话题。）
t_en: (...Still, it doesn't seem like something to bring up now.)

t_ch: （晴霓的生母采翼是驭空一生的挚友。她在那场惨烈的战役中牺牲后，驭空收养了她的女儿。也许这段不堪回首的往事正是驭空的心结。）
t_en: (Qingni's birth mother, Caiyi, was Yukong's lifelong friend. When Caiyi passed away in that devastating war, Yukong adopted her daughter. Perhaps this unbearable memory is the root of Yukong's concerns...)

t_ch: （但这似乎不是现在该提出的话题。）
t_en: (...Still, it doesn't seem like something to bring up now.)

t_ch: （看得出来，驭空在那场惨烈的战役之后，承受了巨大的痛苦。难道她正是因此才不愿让晴霓成为斗舰飞行士的吗？）
t_en: (It's clear that Yukong suffered greatly after that terrible war. Is that why she doesn't want Qingni to be a fighter pilot?)

t_ch: （但这似乎不是现在该提出的话题。）
t_en: (...Still, it doesn't seem like something to bring up now.)

t_ch: 这是…采翼的日记？这东西怎么会在……
t_en: Is that... Caiyi's diary? How did you...?

t_ch: 我明白了。晴霓…对不起，我不能支持你的愿望，因为我已对另外一人发过誓言。
t_en: ...I understand now... I'm sorry, Qingni... I cannot support your dream because I made a promise to someone else.

t_ch: 我不奢望你的原谅，但只希望…你能听听我的辩解。
t_en: I don't expect to gain your forgiveness. I just hope you can... understand my reason.

t_ch: 妈，您在说什么？我们是母女，又怎么谈得上「原谅」和「辩解」？无论您说什么，我都愿意听……
t_en: Mom, what are you talking about? I'm your daughter — our bond is bigger than forgiveness and reason. I'll listen to anything you have to say...

t_ch: 我曾和你一样，对天空怀有憧憬。我知道那是什么感受…在宇宙中航行，四周皆是辽阔的空无，远方尽是难以想象的奇观。
t_en: Just like you, I once longed for the sky. I know what it feels like... to fly through infinite space, to be surrounded by the vast expanse of nothingness, with unimaginable splendors looming in the distance.

t_ch: 在星海之间像一叶孤舟似的漂游，一些人称之为「孤独」，但我们更愿意称之为「自由」。
t_en: To float like a single leaf in the fathomless stellar sea... Some called it "loneliness," but we called it "freedom."

t_ch: 采翼也是这样的人。我们从小便是好友，驾驶着星槎到处惹是生非，直到被人抓到景元将军面前。
t_en: Caiyi was like that, too. We were friends since childhood. We flew starskiffs everywhere — causing trouble until the Knights caught us and dragged us before General Jing Yuan.

t_ch: 不久后，我们就成了天舶司最为精锐的斗舰飞行士。那生活谈不上轻松，甚至称得上残酷——
t_en: We soon became the most elite fighter pilots of the Sky-Faring Commission. It wasn't an easy life... you could even say it was a cruel one.

t_ch: 你永远不会知道身边的朋友能否返航，当然，也不会知道你自己能否返航。
t_en: You never knew if the friend next to you would make it back alive. Of course, the same could be said of yourself.

t_ch: 但对我来说，那是我人生中最为美好的回忆。抛洒热血、搏击长空、荡涤妖邪、匡扶正义，狐人的一生如此短暂，难道不正应为这样光荣的事业而燃烧吗？
t_en: But those are my best memories. We would strike out into the sky — repelling demons and upholding justice — the blood we shed bore witness to it all. The life of a foxian is short and fleeting... surely we should dedicate ourselves to such glorious aims?

t_ch: 今天宙域晴朗！正是适合飞行的好天气！
t_en: System space all clear today! Great weather for flying!

t_ch: 这次是去打步离人的主力舰队，还是别那么激动，稍微冷静些吧。
t_en: We're engaging the main borisin fleet this time. Don't get too excited.

t_ch: 怎么？休了一年产假，技巧迟钝了？没有自信了？
t_en: Huh? Sounds like maternity leave knocked the wind out of your sails! Feeling rusty?

t_ch: 去你的吧。我看你是太久没机会和我一起飞行，现在才这么激动的。是不是想我想得不得了？
t_en: Oh, shut it. I think you're just excited because you haven't flown with me for so long. Did you miss me?

t_ch: 是啊，想死你了。没有你伴航，总觉得银河都小了一圈……
t_en: Of course I missed you! The galaxy felt smaller without you in my cockpit...

t_ch: 说起来，采翼…我真的没想到你还会回来…开斗舰对你而言就这么有吸引力吗？
t_en: Say, Caiyi... I really didn't think you'd come back... Do you really like flying starskiffs that much?

t_ch: 那可太有吸引力了…又苦又累钱又少，天天把脑袋绑在裤腰带上，一年到头也回不了几次罗浮……
t_en: You bet. Sure it's exhausting, dangerous, low-paid, hehe — plus you only get back to the Luofu a few times a year...

t_ch: 可如果让我再选一次，我还是会成为斗舰飞行士……
t_en: ...but given the choice, I'd always pick this...

t_ch: 因为我已经触碰过天空了。
t_en: ...I guess I already touched the sky.

t_ch: 大脑是个奇怪的器官。活了两百多年，我早已忘记了大多数闲谈…但惟有那段无聊的闲谈，我清楚地记得每一句。
t_en: The mind is mysterious... I've lived for more than two hundred years, forgotten nearly every conversation I've ever had... but I still remember us shooting the breeze that day — it's crystal clear.

t_ch: 因为那是我和采翼的最后一次闲谈。
t_en: It was the last time I spoke to Caiyi.

t_ch: 采翼…坚持住…采翼，采翼，睁开眼睛，采翼……
t_en: Caiyi... Hold on, Caiyi... Caiyi, open your eyes... Caiyi...

t_ch: 谢谢你，我度过了很好的一生…但别让晴霓走上这条道路，别让她…成为斗舰飞行士……
t_en: Thank you, Yukong — I've had a good life... but please... don't let Qingni walk the same path... Don't let her... become a fighter pilot...

t_ch: 采翼！那是你的女儿，有什么话你去自己和她说！
t_en: Caiyi, she's your daughter! You'll be able to tell her yourself, do you hear?

t_ch: 救援马上就到了，你睁开眼睛！晴霓还在等你！广渊已经死了，晴霓不能再失去你了！给我醒过来啊！
t_en: The rescue is almost here — stay with me! Qingni is waiting for you! She lost Guangyuan, she can't afford to lose you too! Wake up!!!

t_ch: 采翼！采翼！采翼…采翼……
t_en: Caiyi! Caiyi! Caiyi... Caiyi...

t_ch: 当我从燃烧着熊熊烈火的星槎残骸中爬出，看到的是那颗无血无泪的星球未经大气修饰的天空。
t_en: When I climbed out of the burning carcass of the starskiff, I looked up into a clear sky — pure and seemingly unadorned by the atmosphere that arced far above us.

t_ch: 那是我见过的最美的天空，可我只感到难以忍受的悲伤。我只能像只该死的蚂蚁匍匐在大地上，眼巴巴地望着它。
t_en: It was the most beautiful sky I had ever seen... but all I could feel was unbearable pain. I sunk to the ground and gazed up helplessly.

t_ch: 在梦里，我死在了那天。
t_en: In my dream, that was the day I died.

t_ch: 这是我第一次听您谈起这些事……但是，妈妈，我已经不是懵懂的孩子了。成为斗舰飞行士是一条残酷的道路，这我早有准备……
t_en: Mom... this is the first time I've heard you talk about this... but I'm not a little girl anymore. The road of a fighter pilot is a cruel one, I know that now...

t_ch: 但我不会因此而退缩，我并不害怕自己和她走上同样的道路…如果牺牲能换来仙舟上黎民苍生的幸福，那我甘愿慨然赴死！
t_en: ...but I won't hide from it — I'm not afraid to walk the same road as her. If my sacrifice can bring happiness to the citizens of the Xianzhou, then I'm ready for it!

t_ch: 你真的很像采翼。所以这些年来，你越是优秀，我就越是恐惧。我知道你早就准备好了…但是，我一直都还没准备好……
t_en: ...You are so very similar to Caiyi. The more outstanding you became over the years, the more fearful I turned. I've known for a long time you were ready... but I was not...

t_ch: 你知道吗？那些残酷的事情从未击倒我。我、采翼，还有你的生父广渊，在入伍之初，我们心中就早有准备。
t_en: You know, the cruelty has never claimed victory. Me, Caiyi, your father Guangyuan... we were prepared for the worst when we enlisted.

t_ch: 可你知道，那场夺走了采翼…夺走了几十万战友的战役…我们最终是如何取得胜利的吗？
t_en: But do you know how we won that war? The war that took away Caiyi and the lives of hundreds of thousands of fighters?

t_ch: 是…帝弓司命的神矢……
t_en: ...The Reignbow Arbiter's divine arrow...

t_ch: 是啊！帝弓司命的浩荡神恩，只用一击就荡平了那群孽物！可仅为了阻挡它们的前进，我们就付出了几十万条生命的代价！
t_en: Yes! The mighty blessing of the Reignbow Arbiter annihilated all abominations in a single strike! But we had to pay with the blood of hundreds of thousands of soldiers just to halt their advance!

t_ch: 如果对于帝弓司命来说，碾碎那些敌人如同呼吸般简单…那么，我们的牺牲又算什么呢？
t_en: If crushing our enemies was as easy as breathing for the Reignbow Arbiter... then what purpose did our sacrifices serve?

t_ch: 在星神的伟力之下，凡人的牺牲，都像是笑话一样…什么都不是…什么都不是……
t_en: Under the might of an Aeon, the sacrifices of ordinary creatures are but a joke... we are nothing... we mean nothing...

t_ch: 妈妈……
t_en: Mom...

t_ch: 但是，我不该因为自己的恐惧，就替别人下决定…即使那个人是我的女儿。
t_en: ...But I should never have allowed my fears to compel me to make decisions for another person... even if that person is my daughter.

t_ch: 我粗暴地干涉了你人生的选择。对不起，这是我身为母亲的失职。
t_en: I brazenly interfered with your choices. I am sorry. That is my failure as a mother.

t_ch: 妈妈…不必道歉，真的不必道歉……
t_en: Mom... you don't need to apologize. Really, you don't need to...

t_ch: 明天，我会去帮你办理手续。你会先从一个地勤人员做起，正如我和采翼当年那样。我相信…你会成为一个了不起的斗舰飞行士。
t_en: I'll take you to do the paperwork tomorrow. You'll start as ground crew, just like Caiyi and I did. I believe that one day... you'll make an outstanding fighter pilot.

t_ch: 啊？真的吗！谢谢妈妈！谢谢妈妈！谢谢妈妈！
t_en: Huh? Really!? Thank you, Mom! Thank you, thank you, thank you!!!

t_ch: 我担心你走向我的道路，更担心你走向采翼的道路。但既然这是你的选择…我便愿意全力支持你，直到你后悔为止。
t_en: I was afraid of you following in my footsteps, and more afraid of you taking after Caiyi. But if this is your choice... then I'm willing to support you, even if you live to regret it.

t_ch: 妈妈…我还有最后一个小要求……
t_en: Mom... I have one last request...

t_ch: 说来听听吧。
t_en: Tell me.

t_ch: 我想…和您一起飞行一次……
t_en: I want to... fly with you at least once...

t_ch: 嗯…这就不了吧。我不会再飞了。
t_en: Hehe, I... I'm afraid I can't. I won't be flying anymore.

t_ch: 这…为什么？
t_en: But... why?

t_ch: 因为我已经触碰过天空了。
t_en: Cause... I already touched the sky.

t_ch: 晴霓站在母亲身边，静静地凝望着那个让她满怀热情的地方。
t_en: Qingni stands beside her mother and quietly gazes at the place that fills her with so much passion.

t_ch: 驭空静静地凝望着那个曾让她满怀热情，又让她失去了一切的地方。
t_en: Yukong gazes quietly at the place that once filled her with so much passion and cost her everything.

t_ch: 还是不要打扰她们了。
t_en: Let's not disturb them.

t_ch: 接入机巧鸟
t_en: Connect the cycrane

t_ch: 一会再说吧
t_en: Let's talk about it later

t_ch: 怪物实在是太多了… {NICKNAME}，我们再坚持一下。不出意外的话，云骑应该也快到了。
t_en: There are too many monsters... {NICKNAME}, let's hold out for a little longer — the Cloud Knights should be here soon.

t_ch: 你们快跑吧！这些怪物一时半刻爬不上来！
t_en: Just run! These monsters can't get up here anytime soon!

t_ch: 晴霓小姐，别说傻话。我们不会丢下你的。
t_en: No can do, Miss Qingni — we're not abandoning you.

t_ch: 我们不会丢下你的！
t_en: We won't leave you here!

t_ch: 区区几个孽物，瞧不起谁呢？
t_en: Hey, you think we can't take them?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，不要盲目自信，这很危险。
t_en: Easy, {NICKNAME}, let's not get ahead of ourselves.

t_ch: 杨叔，咱们先撤吧…
t_en: Mr. Yang, let's get out of here...

t_ch: 再坚持一下，我知道你能做到的。
t_en: Hold out a little longer — I know you can do it.

t_ch: 辛苦两位撑持了，让我来助你们一臂之力！
t_en: You two have held out long enough — help is at hand!

t_ch: 云骑！列阵！准备接战！
t_en: Cloud Knights! Formation! Prepare to engage!

t_ch: …您明明知道我有这个天赋！我可以成为罗浮最棒的飞行士，就像您一样！
t_en: ...You know I have the talent to make it! I can become the Luofu's best pilot, just like you were!

t_ch: 天赋？要是没有那两个异邦人，你现在已经死了。偷走星槎，飞上天的那一刻很快乐吧？被孽物包围时呢？
t_en: Talent? You'd be dead if it weren't for those two travelers. Do you think stealing a starskiff and taking to the skies is a show of talent? What about being trapped by abominations?

t_ch: 那一刻你有没有想过，要是你不在了，我会是什么样的心情？
t_en: Did you at any moment think about what I would feel if something happened to you?

t_ch: 多少人想在天舶司混一份清闲的文职？你答应过我，会有始有终做好它。现在你不仅违背了天舶司的规制，还违背了我们母女的约定。
t_en: Do you know how many people dream of a relaxing desk job at the Sky-Faring Commission? You promised me to work responsibly in your post. Not only have you broken the Sky-Faring Commission's regulations, you've broken your promise to me.

t_ch: 妈妈，我说了很多遍，那是情势所迫！我想帮您，想帮天舶司一次，我不想只是坐在桌前每天面对写不完的文件！我不适合干那个……
t_en: I already told you — I had no choice! I want to help you and the Sky-Faring Commission, not sit at my desk dealing with never-ending papers! That job doesn't suit me...

t_ch: 考斗舰飞行士的时候，我的分数是最高的。他们夸我是不亚于您的天才，都说我不愧是您的女儿，令人羡慕……
t_en: I got the highest grade in the fighter pilot test — they all said I was a genius like you. "Like mother, like daughter." They were all jealous of me...

t_ch: 可他们都不知道，我是偷着去考的，因为如果被您知道了，您绝不会允许！
t_en: But no one even knew I had to take the test behind your back. You would never have allowed me to go if I told you about it!

t_ch: 偷着去考？当时我要是说个「不」字，你以为你能走进考场，坐上斗舰？
t_en: Behind my back? Do you really think you could've walked into the test and sat in the cockpit if I hadn't given my permission behind the scenes?

t_ch: 至于「天才」，别在我跟前提起这两个字。
t_en: As for "genius," don't mention that word to me again.

t_ch: 我不明白，你为什么一定要成为飞行士！
t_en: Why do you insist on becoming a pilot!? I don't understand.

t_ch: 我也想不通，您为什么就是不明白……
t_en: And I don't understand why you don't understand...

t_ch: 这世上任何人都能做斗舰飞行士，唯独司舵的女儿不能！怎么会有这么荒唐的事！您自己不愿意再飞，也不该捆住我啊！我不是你养在笼子里的鸟儿！
t_en: Why can anyone in the world become a fighter pilot, except the helm master's daughter? Don't you realize how ridiculous that is? You may not want to fly anymore, but don't trap me down here with you!

t_ch: 晴霓！你要去干什么！
t_en: Qingni! Where are you going?

t_ch: 让二位见笑了。
t_en: *sigh* I'm sorry you had to witness this.

t_ch: 这是我第一次听您谈起这些事……但是，妈妈，我已经不是懵懂的孩子了。成为斗舰飞行士是一条残酷的道路，这我早有准备……
t_en: Mom... this is the first time I've heard you talk about this... but I'm not a little girl anymore. The road of a fighter pilot is a cruel one, I know that now...

t_ch: 但我不会因此而退缩，我并不害怕自己和她走上同样的道路…如果牺牲能换来仙舟上黎民苍生的幸福，那我甘愿慨然赴死！
t_en: ...but I won't hide from it — I'm not afraid to walk the same road as her. If my sacrifice can bring happiness to the citizens of the Xianzhou, then I'm ready for it!

t_ch: 你真的很像采翼。所以这些年来，你越是优秀，我就越是恐惧。我知道你早就准备好了…但是，我一直都还没准备好……
t_en: ...You are so very similar to Caiyi. The more outstanding you became over the years, the more fearful I turned. I've known for a long time you were ready... but I was not...

t_ch: 你知道吗？那些残酷的事情从未击倒我。我、采翼，还有你的生父广渊，在入伍之初，我们心中就早有准备。
t_en: You know, the cruelty has never claimed victory. Me, Caiyi, your father Guangyuan... we were prepared for the worst when we enlisted.

t_ch: 可你知道，那场夺走了采翼…夺走了几十万战友的战役…我们最终是如何取得胜利的吗？
t_en: But do you know how we won that war? The war that took away Caiyi and the lives of hundreds of thousands of fighters?

t_ch: 是…帝弓司命的神矢……
t_en: ...The Reignbow Arbiter's divine arrow...

t_ch: 是啊！帝弓司命的浩荡神恩，只用一击就荡平了那群孽物！可仅只为了阻挡它们的前进，我们就付出了几十万条生命的代价！
t_en: Yes! The mighty blessing of the Reignbow Arbiter annihilated all abominations in a single strike! But we had to pay with the blood of hundreds of thousands of soldiers just to halt their advance!

t_en: If crushing our enemies was as easy as breathing for the Reignbow Arbiter... then what purpose did our sacrifices serve?

t_ch: 在星神的伟力之下，凡人的牺牲，都像是笑话一样…什么都不是…什么都不是……
t_en: Under the might of an Aeon, the sacrifices of ordinary creatures are but a joke... we are nothing... we mean nothing...

t_ch: 妈妈……
t_en: Mom...

t_ch: 但是，我不该因为自己的恐惧，就替别人下决定…即使那个人是我的女儿。
t_en: ...But I should never have allowed my fears to compel me to make decisions for another person... even if that person is my daughter.

t_en: I brazenly interfered with your choices. I am sorry. That is my failure as a mother.

t_ch: 妈妈…不必道歉，真的不必道歉……
t_en: Mom... you don't need to apologize. Really, you don't need to...

t_en: I'll take you to fill in the paperwork tomorrow. You'll start as ground crew, just like Caiyi and I did. I believe that one day... you'll make an outstanding fighter pilot.

t_ch: 啊？真的吗！谢谢妈妈！谢谢妈妈！谢谢妈妈！
t_en: Huh? Really!? Thank you, Mom! Thank you, thank you, thank you!!!

t_ch: 我担心你走向我的道路，更担心你走向采翼的道路。但既然这是你的选择…我便愿意全力支持你，直到你后悔为止。
t_en: I was afraid of you following in my footsteps, and more afraid of you taking after Caiyi. But if this is your choice... then I'm willing to support you, even if you live to regret it.

t_ch: 妈妈…我还有最后一个小要求……
t_en: Mom... I have one last request...

t_ch: 说来听听吧。
t_en: Tell me.

t_ch: 我想…和您一起飞行一次……
t_en: I want to... fly with you at least once...

t_ch: 嗯…这就不了吧。我不会再飞了。
t_en: Hehe, I... I'm afraid I can't. I won't be flying anymore.

t_ch: 这…为什么？
t_en: But... why?

t_ch: 因为我已经触碰过天空了。
t_en: Cause... I already touched the sky.

t_ch: 机巧鸟连接上手机，就能远程遥控它了。快点用它来寻找失事地点吧。
t_en: Connect the cycrane to your phone to control it remotely. Use it to find the crash, and hurry.

t_ch: 晴霓，再坚持一下！我们这就来救你！
t_en: Hold on, Qingni! We're coming to get you!

t_ch: 你为什么就是不肯听我的？
t_en: Why won't you listen to me?

t_ch: 因为这是我自己的人生！
t_en: Because it's MY life!

t_ch: 等你们做完要做的事情，再来这里找我吧。
t_en: Come find me once you've done what you're here to do.

t_ch: 请收件人输入取件信息。
t_en: Please enter package receipt information.

t_ch: （输入梁沐的信息）
t_en: (Enters Liangmu's information.)

t_ch: 收件人{NICKNAME}已确认，请取走您的包裹。
t_en: Recipient {NICKNAME} confirmed. Please take your package.

t_ch: （应该叫上白露一起打开这个包裹。）
t_en: (I should open it with Bailu.)

t_ch: 等你好久了，是怎样的包裹啊？
t_en: What took you so long? What kind of package is it?

t_ch: （拿出包裹）
t_en: (Take out the package)

t_ch: 这里面有一封信，你帮我打开一下吧。
t_en: There's a letter inside! Help me open it.

t_ch: 白露一把抢走了信纸，撕得稀碎，并且将它扔进了云海里。
t_en: In a flash, Bailu grabs the letter, tears it to pieces, and hurls them into the air.

t_ch: 这个叫梁沐的家伙真是个渣渣。他从来就没有把半夏放在心上，只是想利用她弄到长生药。难道说，半夏是一厢情愿地喜欢着他吗？
t_en: That Liangmu is a complete scumbag — he never cared about Banxia! He only wanted to use her to get to the elixir of immortality... Why couldn't Banxia see it?

t_ch: 早知道就不打开包裹了。
t_en: If we'd known, we wouldn't have opened the package...

t_ch: 对啊，早知道就应该直接扔到哪个垃圾桶里去。
t_en: Right... because if we'd known, it'd be in the trash by now.

t_ch: 幸好半夏她没有看到这封信。
t_en: It's lucky Banxia didn't see the letter.

t_ch: 嗯，没错。
t_en: Yeah, you're right.

t_ch: 让那个混账家伙就这样灰溜溜地滚回他的老家去吧！好后悔啊，后悔治好了这样的渣渣。
t_en: That jerk better run as far as his legs will carry him! Ugh, I regret healing him so much now.

t_ch: …虽然一肚子火，但是现在想想，半夏姐姐和那个人相处那么久，也许她早就知道了。
t_en: ...And it makes me even angrier to think that Miss Banxia might've known what he was like to begin with, but stayed with him all the same.

t_ch: 梁沐肯定欺骗了她！
t_en: No way, Liangmu must've tricked her!

t_ch: 爱情使人盲目。
t_en: Love is blind.

t_ch: 只有她自己知道真相是什么。
t_en: Only she knew the truth.

t_ch: 大人的感情真复杂！我搞不懂，现在也不想搞懂。
t_en: Grownups are so complicated! I don't understand, and I don't want to understand.

t_ch: 真是气得我肚子都饿了。{NICKNAME}，咱们去大吃一顿吧，你请客喔。
t_en: First I was hungry, then I was angry, and now I'm hangry. *sigh* Come on {NICKNAME}, let's get a bite to eat. Your treat!

t_ch: 没、没想到啊…
t_en: Th—That was unexpected...

t_ch: 乱丢垃圾不好…
t_en: I think that counts as littering...

t_ch: 出示卡芙卡的短信
t_en: Show Kafka's message.

t_ch: 是吗？…她还留在仙舟啊。
t_en: Really? So she's still on the Xianzhou.

t_ch: 您认为我该怎么做？
t_en: What do you think I should do?

t_ch: 您能代替我去吗？
t_en: Can you go instead of me?

t_ch: 我吗？我会建议你去一趟。就算你不愿意再和她有什么来往，也最好当面和她说清楚…看这段信息，她也许真的很需要你的帮助。
t_en: Me? I'd suggest you go yourself. Even if you don't want any more dealings with her, it's best to say that to her in person... Reading this message, it seems like she may really need your help.

t_ch: 作为星穹列车的一员，我不想和星核猎手有什么来往；但是{NICKNAME}…她毕竟和你有很深的关联。从过往的经历来看，至少她对你没有恶意。
t_en: As a passenger of the Express, I don't want anything to do with the Stellaron Hunters. But... she does have a deep connection with you, {NICKNAME}. Based on her actions so far, it seems like she has no ill will towards you.

t_ch: 换作是我的话——如果我在危难之际寻求谁的帮助，我会很希望他出现在面前吧……
t_en: If it were me seeking help from someone in a time of crisis, I'd sure hope they showed up in person...

t_ch: 所以，按照你的心意去做就好，{NICKNAME}。我想，卡芙卡不会伤害你的。
t_en: So, {NICKNAME}, do as you see fit. I don't think Kafka intends to do you harm.

t_ch: （不能打草惊蛇，先确认下卡芙卡的位置吧……）
t_en: (Let's not give ourselves away and make sure where Kafka is first...)

t_ch: 你有没有见过这个人？
t_en: Have you seen this person?

t_ch: （这是…刃的通缉令？仙舟还在缉拿刃吗？）
t_en: (This is... Blade's wanted poster? Is Xianzhou still hunting down Blade?)

t_ch: 妨碍你捉拿卡芙卡了，真对不起。
t_en: Sorry for getting in your way when you were going after Kafka.

t_ch: …你在说什么啊，那不是一次误报么？你也不是故意的…嗨，我没放在心上。
t_en: ...What are you talking about? Wasn't that just a communication error? You didn't mean it. I don't mind.

t_ch: （卡芙卡用言灵给他植入了暗示，他不记得那件事了。）
t_en: (Kafka used Spirit Whisper to implant suggestions into him. He doesn't remember anymore.)

t_ch: 出示卡芙卡的短信
t_en: Show Kafka's message.

t_ch: …卡芙卡和刃还在仙舟？将军近乎明示放他们走了，可那是当时的事。没记错的话，仙舟只撤销了卡芙卡的通缉令，刃还是重犯……
t_en: ...Kafka and Blade are still on the Xianzhou? The general nearly released them outright, but that was then. If memory serves, the Xianzhou only revoked Kafka's arrest warrant. Blade is still a wanted criminal...

t_ch: 您认为我该怎么做？
t_en: What do you think I should do?

t_ch: 您觉得她是何居心？
t_en: What do you think her motive is?

t_ch: 说真的，我搞不清楚星核猎手，尤其那个女人的想法……
t_en: To be honest, I can never figure out what those Stellaron Hunters are up to, especially that Kafka...

t_ch: 但直觉告诉我，她不会做伤害你的事情。如果她真的遇到了麻烦，还是去见见她比较好。
t_en: ...but my intuition tells me she won't do anything to harm you. If she's really in trouble, it'd be best to go see her.

t_ch: （居然藏在太卜司…胆子真大。）
t_en: (Hiding out at the Divination Commission... that's a bold decision.)

t_ch: （就在这附近了……）
t_en: (She should be around here somewhere...)

t_ch: 我知道你一定会来的，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Hm, I knew you would come, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 如果拥有艾利欧那样的眼睛，这世界就不再是你我现在看见的样子；无数可能性交叠在一起，代表不同选择所引致的后果。而每一刻，无穷多的选择都不断收束成同一个点，成为现在，成为过去……
t_en: If we had Elio's eyes, the world would not appear as it does to you and I. There are myriad possibilities overlapping one another, representing the consequences of different choices. In every passing moment, an infinite number of choices converge on the same point and become the present, and then become the past...

t_ch: 你的选择是这样吗？接受我的请求，来帮助我？
t_en: So... was this your choice? To respond to my request for help?

t_ch: 嗯，毕竟你需要我的帮助。
t_en: Yes, it sounded like you needed some.

t_ch: 是的，阿刃的情况很棘手，在这仙舟上…我能拜托的只有你了。
t_en: I do, Blade is in a prickly situation. On the Xianzhou... you're the only one I can turn to.

t_ch: 我还没有决定。
t_en: I haven't made my choice yet.

t_ch: 是吗？那要怎么让你下定决心呢？
t_en: Is that so? Haha, so how can we get you to make up your mind?

t_ch: 不，我是来拒绝你的。
t_en: No, I'm here to refuse your request.

t_ch: 咦…真的吗？你会拒绝我吗？
t_en: Huh... Really? You refuse?

t_ch: 如果你想要拒绝我的话，此时此刻，你为什么会在这里？
t_en: ...If you wanted to refuse, then why would you be standing here right now?

t_ch: 你说得对…我确实想帮助你。
t_en: You have a point... I'm here to help you.

t_ch: 谢谢。在这仙舟上…我能拜托的只有你了。
t_en: I'm grateful. On the Xianzhou... you're the only one I can turn to.

t_ch: 因为我要来当面说「不」。
t_en: So I could say "no" to your face.

t_ch: 这样的可能性也许存在…可是，{NICKNAME}，你会做出那样的选择吗？如果你现在走开，随后发生的一切都将与你无关。我对你抱持的期望与需要，也都会化为乌有。
t_en: Such a possibility may exist... but, {NICKNAME}, is that a choice you would make? If you walk away now, everything that happens afterwards will not involve you. All the expectations and needs that I have for you would also vanish into thin air.

t_ch: 因为我没有选择…
t_en: Because I have no choice...

t_ch: 是啊，我们都是…「命运的奴隶」。
t_en: Haha, yes, we're all "Destiny's Slave."

t_ch: 被称为「命运」的东西，既看不见，也摸不着；可是，它就像所有坍塌收束的过去和现在一样确凿无疑。我呼唤你，而你来了；你拥有许许多多的选择，而一切都将你导向这里…此时此地。
t_en: What we call "destiny" is neither visible nor tangible, yet it is as certain as the collapsing and converging of the past and present. I called out to you, and you came. You had many choices, but everything led you here... to right here and right now.

t_ch: 你应我的召唤而来，也知道我是真的需要你的帮助，可你却在犹豫，为什么？
t_en: You heard the call and knew that I truly did need your help, so why are you hesitating?

t_ch: 我想了解情况…
t_en: I want to understand the situation...

t_ch: 可是，我已经都告诉你了。
t_en: But I've already told you.

t_ch: 我只是讨厌你。
t_en: I dislike you.

t_ch: 是吗……
t_en: Is that so...

t_ch: 我不相信命运。
t_en: I don't believe in destiny.

t_ch: 你相信这个宇宙是自由的吗？
t_en: So you believe that this universe is free?

t_ch: 好吧，那样的话，你就保留这一份选择好了——{NICKNAME}，你会留下来吗？为了我。
t_en: Well, in that case, how about this choice... {NICKNAME}, will you stay here? For me?
